name: Maryland Infants and Toddlers Program (MITP)
description: Maryland's early intervention program providing free services to children birth to three with developmental delays or disabilities and their families. Operates through 24 local programs statewide. Research shows 68% of children who receive early intervention avoid needing special education by third grade.

name: The Arc Maryland
description: The largest statewide nonprofit organization dedicated to the rights and quality of life of persons with intellectual and developmental disabilities (I/DD) and their families, with 8,000 members and nine local chapters. Provides advocacy, residential, educational, and vocational services.
address: 8601 Robert Fulton Drive, Suite 140, Columbia, MD 21046

name: Special Olympics Maryland
description: Provides year-round sports training and athletic competition in Olympic-type sports for children and adults with intellectual disabilities. Operates across multiple counties with programs entirely run by volunteers. Raises over $3.5 million annually for athletes.

name: Disability Rights Maryland
description: Maryland's designated Protection and Advocacy agency, providing free legal services and advocacy to persons with disabilities. Services include advice, counseling, negotiation, administrative remedies, and litigation, available in English, Spanish, and American Sign Language.

name: Maryland Division of Rehabilitation Services (DORS)
description: An agency of the Maryland State Department of Education helping people with disabilities prepare for, find, and keep jobs. Includes the Workforce and Technology Center in Baltimore offering career assessment, skills training, job placement, and assistive technology services.
ageRange: Working age adults and transition-age youth

name: NAMI Maryland
description: Grassroots organization uniting over 60,000 members dedicated to advocacy, education, and support for persons with mental illness and their families. Offers education workshops, teleconferences, support groups, and campaigns to reduce stigma.

name: Maryland Technology Assistance Program (MDTAP)
description: A program of the Maryland Department of Disabilities providing AT device libraries, demonstrations, consultations, short-term device loans, a Reuse Center for free gently-used equipment, and a low-interest AT loan program ($500-$70,000).

name: Maryland ABLE
description: Maryland's ABLE savings program helping individuals with disabilities save for qualified disability expenses tax-free without jeopardizing SSI or Medicaid. Minimum $25 to open, $1 minimum subsequent contributions, and $35 annual maintenance fee.

name: Kennedy Krieger Institute
description: Johns Hopkins Hospital affiliate providing inpatient and outpatient care, community services, and school programs for children with learning disabilities, brain and spinal cord disorders, and developmental disabilities. Treats more than 19,000 children annually with specialized developmental-behavioral pediatrics.

name: Baltimore Adapted Recreation and Sports (BARS)
description: Community-based nonprofit providing year-round adapted sports programs for individuals of all ages with disabilities and their families in the Baltimore/Washington area. Activities include aquatics, baseball, soccer, softball, and water skiing.
